channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Google says 90 zero-days were exploited in attacks last year
item.description: Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) tracked 90 zero-day vulnerabilities actively exploited throughout 2025, almost half of them in enterprise software and appliances. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 2026 Browser Data Reveals Major Enterprise Security Blind Spots
item.description: The browser is becoming the operating system for modern work, yet many enterprises still treat it as an extension of network or endpoint security. Keep Aware's 2026 State of Browser Security Report sh...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: DDR5 Bot Scalping, Samsung TV Tracking, Reddit Privacy Fine & More
item.description: Some weeks in cybersecurity feel routine. This one doesn’t. Several new developments surfaced over the past few days, showing how quickly the threat landscape keeps shifting. Researchers uncovered fre...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Preparing for the Quantum Era: Post-Quantum Cryptography Webinar for Security Leaders
item.description: Most organizations assume encrypted data is safe. But many attackers are already preparing for a future where today’s encryption can be broken. Instead of trying to decrypt information now, they are c...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Police dismantles online gambling ring exploiting Ukrainian women
item.description: Spanish and Ukrainian law enforcement authorities dismantled a criminal ring that exploited war-displaced Ukrainian women to run an online gambling scheme that laundered nearly €4.75 million in illici...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dust Specter Targets Iraqi Officials with New SPLITDROP and GHOSTFORM Malware
item.description: A suspected Iran-nexus threat actor has been attributed to a campaign targeting government officials in Iraq by impersonating the country's Ministry of Foreign Affairs to deliver a set of never-before...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Where Multi-Factor Authentication Stops and Credential Abuse Starts
item.description: Organizations typically roll out multi-factor authentication (MFA) and assume stolen passwords are no longer enough to access systems. In Windows environments, that assumption is often wrong. Attacker...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cisco flags more SD-WAN flaws as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: ​Cisco has flagged two Catalyst SD-WAN Manager security flaws as actively exploited in the wild, urging administrators to upgrade vulnerable devices. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: APT28-Linked Campaign Deploys BadPaw Loader and MeowMeow Backdoor in Ukraine
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Russian cyber campaign that has targeted Ukrainian entities with two previously undocumented malware families named BadPaw and MeowMeow. "The ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Phobos ransomware admin pleads guilty to wire fraud conspiracy
item.description: A Russian national pleaded guilty to a wire fraud conspiracy charge related to his role in administering the Phobos ransomware operation, which breached hundreds of victims worldwide. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Europol-Led Operation Takes Down Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Linked to 64,000 Attacks
item.description: Tycoon 2FA, one of the prominent phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) toolkits that allowed cybercriminals to stage adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) credential harvesting attacks at scale, was dismantled by a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI and Europol Seize LeakBase Forum Used to Trade Stolen Credentials
item.description: A joint law enforcement operation has dismantled LeakBase, one of the world's largest online forums for cybercriminals to buy and sell stolen data and cybercrime tools. The LeakBase forum, per the U.S...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Bitwarden adds support for passkey login on Windows 11
item.description: Bitwarden announced support for logging into Windows 11 devices using passkeys stored in the manager's vault, enabling phishing-resistant authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mail2Shell zero-click attack lets hackers hijack FreeScout mail servers
item.description: A maximum severity vulnerability in the FreeScout helpdesk platform allows hackers to achieve remote code execution without any user interaction or authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: VMware Aria Operations Bug Exploited, Cloud Resources at Risk
item.description: Exploitation of the command injection flaw in VMware Aria Operations could grant an attacker broad acess to victims' cloud environments....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Windows 10 KB5075039 update fixes broken Recovery Environment
item.description: Microsoft has released the KB5075039 Windows Recovery Environment update for Windows 10 to fix a long-standing issue that prevented some users from accessing the Recovery environment. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Fake LastPass support email threads try to steal vault passwords
item.description: Password management software provider LastPass is warning users of a phishing campaign targeting its users with fake unauthorized account access alerts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cisco warns of max severity Secure FMC flaws giving root access
item.description: Cisco has released security updates to patch two maximum-severity vulnerabilities in its Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC) software. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Spyware-grade Coruna iOS exploit kit now used in crypto theft attacks
item.description: A previously undocumented set of 23 iOS exploits named "Coruna" has been deployed by multiple threat actors in targeted espionage campaigns and financially motivated attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hacker mass-mails HungerRush extortion emails to restaurant patrons
item.description: Customers of restaurants using the HungerRush point-of-sale (POS) platform say they received emails from a threat actor attempting to extort the company, warning that restaurant and customer data coul...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FBI seizes LeakBase cybercrime forum, data of 142,000 members
item.description: The FBI has seized the LeakBase cybercrime forum, a major online forum used by cybercriminals buy and sell hacking tools and stolen data. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hac...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stranger Things Meets Cybersecurity: Lessons from the Hive Mind
item.description: Events and concepts from the Stranger Things television series illustrate how enterprises can defend their networks and stay "right side up."...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Europol-coordinated action disrupts Tycoon2FA phishing platform
item.description: An international law enforcement operation coordinated by Europol has disrupted Tycoon2FA, a major phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform linked to tens of millions of phishing messages each month. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Mississippi medical center reopens clinics hit by ransomware attack
item.description: The University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) says it has resumed normal operations, nine days after a ransomware attack blocked access to electronic medical records and took down many of its IT...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million,...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five fu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroo...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: China's Silver Dragon Razes Governments in EU, SE Asia
item.description: The emerging actor, part of the APT41 nexus, gains initial access via phishing, and uses legitimate network services to obscure cyber espionage activities....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading
